definition: Build a hierarchical navigable small-world graph over training embeddings and query evaluation embeddings for approximate top-k train neighbors. Use the returned edges as SPECTRA pairwise similarities after measuring recall on an exact subset.

scale_profile.best_for: Large dense embedding tables where exact train-eval matrix multiplication is too expensive.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Hundreds of thousands to many millions of vectors on a single machine, depending on memory.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The audit requires exact low-similarity tails rather than nearest-neighbor overlap.

tunable_parameters.name: k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of train neighbors retained per evaluation unit.

tunable_parameters.name: M
tunable_parameters.purpose: HNSW graph degree controlling memory and recall.

quality_gates: Measure recall@k against exact search on a representative subset.
Report HNSW parameters and whether vectors were normalized.
Check that nearest-neighbor coverage remains adequate in the most novel eval region.
failure_modes: Approximate misses can make samples look spuriously distant from train.
Embedding similarity may encode leakage if embeddings were trained with labels or test data.
Memory grows with graph degree and vector count.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py for an exact local prototype, then replace its scorer with FAISS/HNSW while keeping the same CSV columns.

citations.title: Efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search using Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs
citations.evidence: Introduces HNSW as a multi-layer graph for approximate nearest-neighbor search with high recall and efficient routing.

citations.title: ANN-Benchmarks: A Benchmarking Tool for Approximate Nearest Neighbor Algorithms
citations.evidence: Frames ANN selection as a quality/performance tradeoff and evaluates recall for k-nearest-neighbor methods.
